questions.question: A standalone component uses *ngIf in its template. What must appear in its imports array?
options: Nothing — *ngIf is a built-in Angular directive that's always available
CommonModule (or NgIf directly), because standalone components declare their own dependencies
BrowserModule, because *ngIf is part of the browser platform
FormsModule, because ngIf is related to form control

questions.question: You want to add a standalone component to an existing NgModule-based app. Where does it go in @NgModule?
options: declarations — same as any other component
imports — standalone components are imported, not declared
providers — standalone components register themselves as services
exports only — never in imports or declarations

questions.question: What does bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, appConfig) replace in a modern Angular app?
options: The app.component.ts file
platformBrowserDynamic().bootstrapModule(AppModule) from main.ts
The index.html entry point
The angular.json build configuration

questions.question: What is the advantage of loadComponent over loadChildren for lazy loading?
options: loadComponent is faster at runtime because it skips the router
loadComponent lazy-loads a single standalone component without requiring a NgModule or routes wrapper
loadChildren is deprecated; loadComponent is its direct replacement in all cases
loadComponent supports preloading; loadChildren does not

questions.question: In app.config.ts, which function replaces importing HttpClientModule in AppModule?
options: withFetch()
importProvidersFrom(HttpClientModule)
provideHttpClient()
enableHttpClient()
